Description: As a master user, I can register with phone and verify OTP before account creation.
Acceptance Criteria: OTP verified users can create accounts; invalid OTP attempts are rate-limited; audit log is written.

Description: As compliance, I can trigger KYC checklist based on user country of residence.
Acceptance Criteria: Country profile drives required fields; submission status is stored; pending users cannot transact.

Description: As compliance, I can screen onboarding profiles against watchlists.
Acceptance Criteria: Screening runs on onboarding and updates; flagged accounts move to manual review queue.

Description: As ops, I can review, approve, reject, and comment on KYC cases.
Acceptance Criteria: Queue supports filters; decisions are timestamped; reviewer identity is stored.

Description: As a master user, I can link an attached family account.
Acceptance Criteria: Master can create attached users; relationship appears in both account views.

Description: As a master user, I can assign permissions to an attached user.
Acceptance Criteria: Permissions enforced at transaction time; unauthorized actions are blocked.

Description: As a master user, I can set daily and per-transaction limits.
Acceptance Criteria: Limit checks happen before authorization; limit updates are audited.

Description: As system, I can create wallets with currency sub-accounts.
Acceptance Criteria: Wallet creation is idempotent; balances start at zero; wallet state is queryable.

Description: As system, every monetary event posts balanced debit and credit entries.
Acceptance Criteria: Entries are balanced; posting is atomic; each entry has channel and reference IDs.

Description: As system, I can place and release holds for pending operations.
Acceptance Criteria: Holds reduce available balance; release/consume updates are auditable.

Description: As ops, I can reverse failed or duplicate transactions safely.
Acceptance Criteria: Reversal keeps ledger balanced; original reference is linked; approval is logged.

Description: As system, I ingest and cache FX rates from configured providers.
Acceptance Criteria: Rates refresh on schedule; stale rates are flagged; source metadata is stored.

Description: As user, I can see rate, spread, fee, and payout amount before confirmation.
Acceptance Criteria: Quote has expiry; fee/spread are versioned; response includes final receivable amount.

Description: As system, cross-currency transfers post correctly to ledger.
Acceptance Criteria: Conversion uses accepted quote; gain/loss fields are logged for reconciliation.

Description: As users, I can transfer between Beelapay wallets.
Acceptance Criteria: Transfers are idempotent; status lifecycle is stored; balances update atomically.

Description: As master, I can transfer to linked attached users quickly.
Acceptance Criteria: Linked recipients are selectable; limit checks apply; success triggers SMS.

Description: As users, I can view recent transactions and statuses.
Acceptance Criteria: History supports paging and filters; each item shows amount, channel, and status.

Description: As feature-phone users, I can authenticate and navigate core menus.
Acceptance Criteria: Session timeout works; PIN validation is enforced; retries are rate-limited.

Description: As feature-phone users, I can check balance and recent transactions.
Acceptance Criteria: Balance returns available funds; mini-statement shows latest transactions.

Description: As feature-phone users, I can send money using USSD steps.
Acceptance Criteria: Send flow confirms recipient and amount; success and failure return explicit message.

Description: As ops, I can onboard a merchant and assign merchant identifier.
Acceptance Criteria: Merchant profile status includes pending, active, suspended; changes are audited.

Description: As customer, I can pay a merchant through selected channel.
Acceptance Criteria: Authorization checks balance and limits; merchant receives confirmation.

Description: As ops, I can track merchant payment settlement lifecycle.
Acceptance Criteria: Each payment has settlement state; settlement exceptions are visible in queue.

Description: As system, I can send payout requests to one external partner rail.
Acceptance Criteria: Adapter handles request signing, retries, and timeout policy.

Description: As system, I can process async status callbacks idempotently.
Acceptance Criteria: Duplicate callbacks do not double-post; terminal status updates transaction state.

Description: As ops, I can review and resolve failed payout cases.
Acceptance Criteria: Exception queue includes failure codes, retries, and manual resolution notes.

Description: As users, I receive OTP via SMS for login and sensitive actions.
Acceptance Criteria: OTP delivery is logged; resend policy enforced; expired OTPs rejected.

Description: As users, I receive SMS confirmations for balance-changing events.
Acceptance Criteria: Every successful transfer/payment triggers SMS; template variables render correctly.

Description: As master, I get alerts when attached users spend funds.
Acceptance Criteria: Alert includes attached user alias, amount, and merchant/reference.

Description: As ops, I can search transactions by user, phone, or reference ID.
Acceptance Criteria: Search returns full status timeline and linked ledger references.

Description: As finance, I can reconcile internal ledger against partner settlement data.
Acceptance Criteria: Daily report produced; mismatches are categorized and queued for review.

Description: As compliance, I can flag high-risk transaction patterns for review.
Acceptance Criteria: Rules generate cases with severity and reason; case actions are audited.

Description: As master users, I can enable MFA for account protection.
Acceptance Criteria: MFA challenge applies to sensitive actions; backup recovery path exists.

Description: As audit/compliance, I have immutable records of critical actions.
Acceptance Criteria: Critical events are append-only; event schema includes actor, time, and context.

Description: As engineering, I can monitor failures, latency, and partner rail health.
Acceptance Criteria: Dashboards and alerts exist for core APIs, queue failures, and payout latency.
